title: CI: fix search-test flakes
body:
Seeing constant e2e test failures today, in search tests.

Reason: tests are searching for "alpine", which is common,
and we're hitting pollution.

Solution: search for "testdigest_v2s<x>", an image in quay
under the libpod namespace. And, in other tests that rely
on docker.io, switch to quay.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>
```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I was wrong in scrum today. Today's test storm is NOT related to registry.redhat, it's another instance where searching for "alpine" returns hits that are not under our control. We keep hitting this (eg #14196), so try to reduce our risk.

I left two instances of `docker.io` in the tests, because I can't find a way to do them in quay:
* search --filter stars=10
* search --filter is-official

Both are flaking.

@containers/podman-maintainers: can someone with superuser access to `quay.io/podman` please see if there's a way to add "stars" or set the "official" bit on the `stable` image? I have access to `libpod` but don't see a way to set either. I don't know if quay supports those?

> @containers/podman-maintainers: can someone with superuser access to `quay.io/podman` please see if there's a way to add "stars" or set the "official" bit on the `stable` image? I have access to `libpod` but don't see a way to set either. I don't know if quay supports those?

AFAIK both of these options are dockerhub specific and not support by other registries, at least the man page says
> --compatible
>
>After the name and the description, also show the stars, official and automated descriptors as Docker does. Podman does not show these descriptors by default since they are not supported by most public container registries.
Fair enough. Then we remain at the mercy of `docker.io`. There is still some value to this PR, in the sense that searching for `alpine` is fragile, but outages like today's will still block us.

Any replacement of docker.io with quay.io SGTM
/lgtm
